Title: Identification of ST1 reveals a selection involving hitchhiking of seed morphology and oil content during soybean domestication

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: I locus (anti-CHS1/CHS cluster-associated seed coat color locus)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The I locus inversion controlling seed coat color resides in the same selective sweep region as ST1. 2. Transgenic introduction of EST Gm-c1069-6017 causing post-transcriptional silencing of the I locus changed seed coat pigmentation. 3. Alteration of the I locus did not significantly affect seed shape or oil content, showing it is not causal for nutrient metabolism traits measured here.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No significant effect on seed oil content or seed shape; affects seed coat color only in this study
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally tested non-pathway/non-regulatory locus because it was directly manipulated to test whether hitchhiking region effects on oil/shape were due to I locus rather than ST1.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic introduction of EST Gm-c1069-6017; phenotyping of seed coat color, seed shape, and oil content
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mainly for seed coat color manipulation; should not be expected to alter oil content based on this study
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Extensive epigenetic and post-transcriptional silencing mechanism involvement noted from prior studies; this paper used anti-CHS1 silencing construct but did not newly dissect mechanism
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Soybean genomic fragment/anti-CHS1 construct in pTF101-Gene replacing 35S promoter
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Selective sweep/structural variation comparison using resequencing and de novo assemblies; no accession number reported

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage oil contributing to nutritional/industrial oil value; pectin contributes to cell wall formation and seed morphology
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Seed oil (triacylglycerol); pectin precursor UDP-galacturonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ST1 was cloned as Glyma.08g109100 controlling seed thickness/roundness in soybean. 2. ST1 overexpression/transgenic complementation increased seed length, width, thickness, 100-seed weight, and oil content, while CRISPR knockout reduced seed size. 3. ST1 converts UDP-glucuronic acid to UDP-galacturonic acid, promoting pectin biosynthesis and increasing cell number/size in seeds. 4. ST1 transgenic seeds showed elevated glycerol-3-phosphate, triglyceride, and oil content, with RNA-seq indicating upregulation of glycolysis-related genes. 5. A C-to-T coding polymorphism/haplotype was associated with round seeds and higher oil content and underwent selection during domestication.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper experimentally supports a direct enzymatic role in UDP-galacturonic acid production/pectin biosynthesis and an indirect positive effect on oil accumulation through altered glycolysis-related transcription/metabolites; the oil effect is not shown as direct catalysis in lipid biosynthesis. ST1 lies within a selective sweep linked to the I locus inversion controlling seed coat color, suggesting hitchhiking during domestication.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream precursor-supply step for pectin biosynthesis; indirect upstream modulator of oil accumulation via glycolysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis; native genomic locus with 2.5 kb upstream promoter in soybean
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase rounder/larger seeds and elevate seed oil content in soybean breeding or engineering; favorable ST1^T allele can be selected or edited, but linkage/hitchhiking with seed coat color domestication region should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seeds; RNA-seq/WGCNA seed-stage data implicated correlation with seed thickness
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing of 302- and 1209-accession soybean panels; RNA-seq of developing seeds (R5/R6/R7 for GR8836 and XHD; R6 for transgenic and non-transgenic Kefeng 1); WGCNA; GWAS; no accession number reported
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Kefeng 1; Williams 82; GR8836; Xiaoheidou (XHD); W1112; also Arabidopsis Col-0 for heterologous test
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seeds, especially R5-R7 seed stages examined; correlated with seed thickness in seed transcriptome modules
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: GR8836; Williams 82 reference haplotypes also analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; fine mapping/positional cloning; transgenic overexpression/complementation in soybean and Arabidopsis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in soybean; metabolite assays; enzyme activity assay; histology; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty reported; likely pleiotropic effects on seed morphology and linked domestication traits
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms precursor for pectin biosynthesis; indirectly supplies/promotes metabolic state favoring seed oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epimerizes UDP-D-glucuronic acid to UDP-D-galacturonic acid, supporting pectin precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cell wall pectin precursor biosynthesis; indirect connection to glycolysis/oil biosynthesis module
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth in Wuhan and Guangzhou for GWAS/phenotyping; greenhouse growth for transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes rounder/thicker and larger seeds and increases seed oil content
